title: Is Asuka the character of the comic relief
display_title: Is Asuka the character of the comic relief
wikitext:
[]: Is Asuka the character of the comic-relief?             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Wed Jan 12, 2011 7:21 pm It's an interesting idea to ponder; essel (the guy who told me this) says that because Asuka does all of these silly gestures sometimes in the show, that makes her a comic relief (or something of the sort, I'm sure he'll expand what he meant soon enough). Personally, I think that's generalizing some of her character throughout the first half, and it definitely contradicts who she became in the second half (although he's right that she does do comic moments throughout the show; he posted me a scene in episode 16 about that, some of the more silly things in episode 8 and 9, the majority of episode 10), but hey, maybe this can be a good excuse to see Asuka's character in a whole new light. So what do you guys think?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Jan 12, 2011 7:23 pm Uh... no. A comic relief character would be more along the lines of Kensuke or Pen Pen.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Jan 12, 2011 7:25 pm Pen Pen is comic relief character Kensuke and Touji come next

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Wed Jan 12, 2011 7:28 pm I feel that the episodes of the action arc are the only times we could percieve Asuka's behaviour as being comical - and I should note that most of the instances in question involve Shinji reacting to her behaviour in an embarrassed, annoyed or confused manner, and it's those reactions that produce a bit of a chuckle or a smile from me.

[]: Re: Is Asuka the character of the comic-relief?             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Jan 12, 2011 7:32 pm I kind of wish you hadn't posted a thread about this, or at least that you had asked before posting to tell everyone I was going to write up a post to explain my position. I don't really feel like digging around for 'evidence' to support my opinion here; take it or leave it. Anyway, C.A.P. wrote: It's an interesting idea to ponder; essel (the guy who told me this) [...] it definitely contradicts who she became in the second half

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 12, 2011 8:07 pm For whatever it's worth, I recall reading articles on NGE that both Misato and Asuka function as comic relief characters (of sorts) for the first half of the series, considering the second half gives them much darker facets to their personalities. If I find the source(s) of that, I'll be sure to post them.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Jan 12, 2011 9:01 pm I think Asuka and Shinji interacting was the comic relief. She bullies him and he make weak attempts at retaliation. Its comic relief and at times fanservice.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Jan 12, 2011 9:10 pm The fan reaction to Eva is the comic relief.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Jan 12, 2011 9:11 pm Allemann wrote: The fan reaction to Eva is the comic relief.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Jan 13, 2011 12:41 am Most of the central cast can be comic relief (except Rei) when the situation calls for it. Asuka and Misato get more comedic moments than the others because of their personalities. But Pen-Pen is truly the only pure comic relief in the series. Even Toji and Kensuke have their serious moments.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Thu Jan 13, 2011 12:41 am I doubt it. She can be a comedic character, but her role in the story goes far beyond just being a reliable source of laughter. Remember, she was responsible for many of the more emotionally wrenching moments of the series as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Sanger Zonvolt » Thu Jan 13, 2011 12:43 am Allemann wrote: The fan reaction to Eva is the comic relief.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jan 13, 2011 11:16 am I don't see her being used for significantly more comic relief than the other characters in the action arc - she just has more screentime, but her fun stuff/serious stuff ratio should be below that of Kaji and the stooges.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jan 13, 2011 12:03 pm Xard wrote: Pen Pen is comic relief character Kensuke and Touji come next

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Thu Jan 13, 2011 2:27 pm No, if anything she's the Chew Toy of the series.

[]:             Quote Post by beaverteeth92 » Thu Jan 13, 2011 3:16 pm I'd say Misato is responsible for the majority of the comic relief. She does, after all, have the most extroverted and overtly-sexual personality.

[]:             Quote Post by Combs » Sat Jan 15, 2011 3:24 am Allemann wrote: The non-fan reaction to Eva is the comic relief.

[]:             Quote Post by Lament » Sat Jan 15, 2011 4:57 pm beaverteeth92 wrote: I'd say Misato is responsible for the majority of the comic relief. She does, after all, have the most extroverted and overtly-sexual personality.

[]:             Quote Post by DevRei17 » Sat Jan 15, 2011 5:05 pm Asuka definitely lightens the mood but only in the first half.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Sat Jan 15, 2011 8:05 pm Shinji does it for me. Everything he did makes me laugh.

Personally, I think that's generalizing some of

Personally, I think that's generalizing some of her character throughout the first half, and it definitely contradicts who she became in the second half (although he's right that she does do comic moments throughout the show; he posted me a scene in episode 16 about that, some of the more silly things in episode 8 and 9, the majority of episode 10), but hey, maybe this can be a good excuse to see Asuka's character in a whole new light. So what do you guys think?
